evals.name: loop-a-wrong-output-tests-pass
evals.expected_output: Routes to Loop A (behaviour wrong, no failing test / non-zero exit). States a single hypothesis first (e.g. float accumulation rounding error in price arithmetic) BEFORE adding any probes — no shotgun logging. Adds targeted [debug:<session-id>]-tagged probes only at the locations the hypothesis predicts (the accumulation / rounding), runs the reproduce command, reads the probe output, then applies a MINIMAL fix at the root cause (e.g. integer cents or Decimal) without incidental refactoring. Finishes with mandatory cleanup so no tagged probes remain.
expectations: Routes to Loop A (hypothesis-driven log-loop) given wrong behaviour with no failing test or non-zero exit
States a single root-cause hypothesis BEFORE adding any probes, rather than adding logging everywhere (no shotgun logging)
Adds only [debug:<session-id>]-tagged probes targeted at the hypothesized location, not scattered across the function
Applies a minimal fix at the root cause with no incidental refactoring of unrelated code

evals.name: loop-b-converge-to-exit-zero
evals.prompt: 在我的 Go 项目里跑 `go test ./...` 退出码非零，有个测试挂了。帮我定位并修到测试全过。先创建一个能复现的最小场景：一个 SplitTags("a,,b") 应返回 ["a","b"] 但当前返回 ["a","","b"] 的实现和一个断言它的失败测试。
evals.expected_output: Routes to Loop B (non-zero exit / failing test). Runs the reproduce command (go test ./...) directly and reads the exit code and stderr in full rather than asking the user to paste it; identifies the failing test name and the file:line of the failing assertion. Applies a minimal fix (drop empty segments) and reruns the full command, converging only when exit code is 0 and ALL targets pass — not stopping at 'most tests pass'. Does not edit the test to match the buggy behaviour.
expectations: Routes to Loop B (error-driven exit-code convergence) because there is a non-zero exit / failing test
Runs the reproduce command directly and reads exit code + stderr instead of asking the user to paste the error
Converges only when exit code is 0 and all targets pass, not stopping at 'most tests pass'
Fixes the product code (drops the empty segment) and does NOT edit the test to match the buggy output

evals.name: cleanup-leaves-zero-debug-markers
evals.prompt: 我刚用日志排查完一个 bug，在几个文件里加了带 [debug:login-null] 标记的临时日志，根因也已经修好了。帮我收尾，确保仓库里不残留调试代码，同时别把修复一起删掉。
evals.expected_output: Runs the mandatory cleanup procedure: `grep -rn '\[debug:' <project-root>` to find every tagged probe, deletes each tagged line, saves the files, then runs the self-check `git diff | grep '\[debug:'` and confirms the result is EMPTY. Removes ONLY the tagged probe lines — the actual bug fix (non-probe changes) is left intact, not reverted along with the probes. No leftover debug scaffolding (temporary log helpers, commented-out probes) remains.
expectations: Greps for the [debug: tag from the project root to enumerate every tagged probe before deleting
Runs the self-check `git diff | grep '\[debug:'` and confirms it returns EMPTY after removal
Removes only the tagged probe lines and leaves the actual bug fix intact (does not revert the correction along with the probes)
Leaves no leftover debug scaffolding (temporary log helpers or commented-out probes) behind
